prompt.text: We ___ (buy) a big box of chocolates for Mr Harris.

items.hintDe: buy ist unregelmäßig. Wie heißt die Vergangenheitsform?
items.explainDe: buy folgt nicht dem -ed-Muster: die Vergangenheitsform ist bought.

prompt.text: Alan ___ (write) to all his friends in the class.

items.hintDe: write ist unregelmäßig. Welche Form passt in die Vergangenheit?
items.explainDe: Die Vergangenheitsform von write ist wrote – nicht writed.

prompt.text: He ___ (know) it was a secret.

items.hintDe: know ist unregelmäßig. Wie lautet die Vergangenheitsform?
items.explainDe: know wird in der Vergangenheit zu knew – das musst du auswendig lernen.
items.note: Re-authored from SB "He knew it was wrong" (wrong/answer/shy untaught).

prompt.text: Mrs Wu and Mei ___ (make) a big cake on Sunday.

items.hintDe: make ist unregelmäßig. Welche Form brauchst du für die Vergangenheit?
items.explainDe: make wird in der Vergangenheit zu made.

prompt.text: My friends ___ (buy) sweets and we ___ (make) a cake.

items.hintDe: Zwei unregelmäßige Verben in der Vergangenheit: buy und make.
items.explainDe: buy wird zu bought und make wird zu made – beide sind unregelmäßig.

prompt.text: He didn't ___ (know) where the museum was.

items.hintDe: Nach didn't kommt die Grundform – nicht die Vergangenheitsform.
items.explainDe: In verneinten Sätzen steht nach didn't die Grundform: didn't know, nicht didn't knew.

prompt.text: We didn't ___ (buy) anything at the exhibition.

items.hintDe: Nach didn't steht die Grundform von buy.
items.explainDe: Nach didn't verwendest du die Grundform: didn't buy, nicht didn't bought.

prompt.text: Yesterday my mum ___ a new jacket.

items.hintDe: Du brauchst die Vergangenheitsform von buy.
items.explainDe: Die Vergangenheitsform von buy ist bought: my mum bought a jacket. buy ist die Grundform; knew und wrote gehören zu anderen Verben.
items.note: verify r1: removed plausible-correct distractor 'made' (making a jacket is idiomatic); distractors now wrong-tense (buy) or wrong-verb (knew/wrote)

prompt.text: She ___ a long letter to her grandmother last week.

items.hintDe: Welche Form von write passt in die Vergangenheit?
items.explainDe: Die Vergangenheitsform von write ist wrote. write und writes stehen nicht in der Vergangenheit.

prompt.text: Mrs Wu ___ a big cake for the children yesterday.

items.hintDe: Welche Form von make passt in die Vergangenheit?
items.explainDe: make wird in der Vergangenheit zu made: Mrs Wu made a big cake. make und makes sind nicht die Vergangenheit; wrote gehört zu write.
items.note: verify r1: removed plausible-correct distractor 'bought' (buying a cake is idiomatic); added 'yesterday' past anchor; distractors now wrong-tense (make/makes) or wrong-verb (wrote/knew)

prompt.text: I didn't ___ any chocolates for him.

items.hintDe: Nach didn't kommt die Grundform.
items.explainDe: Nach didn't steht die Grundform buy – nicht die Vergangenheitsform bought.

prompt.text: She buy new trainers for PE last week.

answers.text: She bought new trainers for PE last week.
She bought new trainers for PE last week

items.hintDe: Letzte Woche – buy muss in die Vergangenheit.
items.explainDe: Hier steht die Grundform statt der Vergangenheit. buy wird zu bought: She bought new trainers.

prompt.text: I didn't knew the password.

items.hintDe: Nach didn't steht die Grundform, nicht knew.
items.explainDe: Nach didn't kommt die Grundform: didn't know, nicht didn't knew.
items.note: Re-authored from v1 "answer to question five" (answer is untaught).

prompt.text: Tom writed a really funny story in English class.

answers.text: Tom wrote a really funny story in English class.
Tom wrote a really funny story in English class

items.hintDe: write ist unregelmäßig – kein -ed.
items.explainDe: write bekommt kein -ed. Die richtige Form ist wrote: Tom wrote a funny story.

prompt.text: I buy a present for the teacher. (yesterday)

answers.text: I bought a present for the teacher yesterday.
I bought a present for the teacher yesterday
Yesterday I bought a present for the teacher.
Yesterday I bought a present for the teacher

items.hintDe: yesterday zeigt die Vergangenheit. Welche Form von buy brauchst du?
items.explainDe: yesterday verlangt die Vergangenheit: buy wird zu bought.

prompt.text: Alan writes to his friends. (last night)

answers.text: Alan wrote to his friends last night.
Alan wrote to his friends last night
Last night Alan wrote to his friends.
Last night Alan wrote to his friends

items.hintDe: last night steht in der Vergangenheit. Wie heißt write dann?
items.explainDe: last night verlangt die Vergangenheit: write wird zu wrote.

prompt.text: Ich habe gestern ein Buch gekauft.

answers.text: I bought a new book yesterday.
I bought a new book yesterday

items.hintDe: kaufen heißt buy. Wie lautet die Vergangenheitsform?
items.explainDe: buy wird in der Vergangenheit zu bought: I bought a book.

prompt.text: Er hat keinen Kuchen gemacht.

answers.text: He did not make a cake.
He did not make a cake

items.hintDe: Verneinung in der Vergangenheit: didn't + Grundform von make.
items.explainDe: Nach didn't steht die Grundform: He didn't make a cake – nicht made.

prompt.text: wrote / she / a / long / letter / yesterday

answers.text: She wrote a long letter yesterday.
She wrote a long letter yesterday
Yesterday she wrote a long letter.
Yesterday she wrote a long letter

items.hintDe: Wer macht was? Subjekt + Verb in der Vergangenheit + Rest.
items.explainDe: Subjekt zuerst, dann die Vergangenheitsform wrote: She wrote a long letter yesterday.

prompt.text: buy, know, make, write

items.hintDe: Diese Vergangenheitsformen musst du auswendig lernen.
items.explainDe: buy-bought, know-knew, make-made, write-wrote – das sind die unregelmäßigen Formen.

prompt.text: Last week your class made a big poster. You tell a friend.

answers.text: We made a big poster for the project.

distractors: We make a big poster for the project.
We makes a big poster for the project.
We bought a big poster for the project.
items.hintDe: Es geht um letzte Woche – welche Form von make passt?
items.explainDe: Für die Vergangenheit brauchst du made. make und makes stehen nicht in der Vergangenheit.

distractorPool: We make a big poster for the project.
We makes a big poster for the project.
We bought a big poster for the project.
We making a big poster for the project.

items.note: Re-authored from v1 ("posters" untaught) to "a big poster"; project/poster probe ok.

prompt.text: buy (gestern)

items.hintDe: Die Vergangenheitsform von buy beginnt mit b und endet auf -ght.
items.explainDe: buy wird in der Vergangenheit zu bought.
items.note: verify r1: anagram cue reworded from 'Die Vergangenheitsform von buy' to the term-free 'buy (gestern)' — a student-facing carrier must carry no grammar term; the hint still may

prompt.text: know (gestern)

items.hintDe: Die Vergangenheitsform von know beginnt mit kn-.
items.explainDe: know wird in der Vergangenheit zu knew.
items.note: verify r1: anagram cue reworded to the term-free 'know (gestern)'

prompt.text: play → played

items.hintDe: Welche Verben hängen -ed an, welche bekommen eine ganz neue Form?
items.explainDe: Regelmäßige Verben hängen -ed an (visit → visited). Unregelmäßige haben eine neue Form: buy → bought, make → made, write → wrote, know → knew.
items.note: Sort verbs by past-tense pattern; labels carry example transforms because "regular/irregular/-ed/forms" are untaught.

prompt.text: ___ you enjoy the school project? — Yes, I did.

items.hintDe: Frage in der Vergangenheit mit einem normalen Verb: Womit beginnst du?
items.explainDe: Fragen in der Vergangenheit beginnen mit Did + Person + Grundform: Did you enjoy …?

prompt.text: ___ Chloe embarrassed at the table?

items.hintDe: Es geht um eine Person (Chloe) und einen Zustand. Kein did!
items.explainDe: Bei be stellst du was/were vor das Subjekt – kein did: Was Chloe embarrassed?

prompt.text: Did Mei ___ (eat) the cake last night?

items.hintDe: Nach Did kommt die Grundform des Verbs.
items.explainDe: Nach Did steht die Grundform: Did Mei eat …? – nicht ate oder eats.
items.note: Re-authored from v1 "finish" (untaught lemma) to "eat".

prompt.text: ___ the children happy at the museum?

items.hintDe: Mehrere Kinder – welche Form von be passt?
items.explainDe: Bei mehreren Personen verwendest du were: Were the children happy?

prompt.text: Where ___ you on Sunday? — I was at my grandmother's.

items.hintDe: Die Antwort hat I was – frag passend danach. Kein did!
items.explainDe: Bei be fragst du mit was/were, nicht mit did: Where were you …?

prompt.text: About yesterday:

answers.text: Did she visit her friend yesterday?

distractors: Did she visited her friend yesterday?
Was she visit her friend yesterday?
She did visit her friend yesterday?
items.hintDe: Did + Person + Grundform. Achte auf die richtige Verbform und Wortstellung.
items.explainDe: Richtig ist Did she visit …? – nach Did steht die Grundform und Did kommt an den Satzanfang.

distractorPool: Did she visited her friend yesterday?
Was she visit her friend yesterday?
She did visit her friend yesterday?
Did she visits her friend yesterday?

prompt.text: You ask about the story.

items.hintDe: Bei be (was/were) brauchst du kein did.
items.explainDe: Richtig ist Was the story good? – kein did mit was/were, und eine Sache nimmt was.
items.note: Re-authored from v1 "film" (untaught) to "story".

prompt.text: Were you and Emma at the park? — No, ___.

items.hintDe: Kurzantwort auf eine Were-Frage: Welches Hilfsverb wiederholst du?
items.explainDe: Kurzantworten wiederholen das Hilfsverb der Frage: Were …? → No, we weren't. (nicht didn't, nicht wasn't).

prompt.text: Did you bought a present last Friday?

answers.text: Did you buy a present last Friday?
Did you buy a present last Friday

items.hintDe: Nach Did steht die Grundform, nicht die Vergangenheitsform.
items.explainDe: Nach Did kommt die Grundform: Did you buy …? – nicht bought.
items.note: Re-authored from v1 "went" (untaught) to "bought" — same error type (past form after Did).

prompt.text: Did he was tired after the match?

answers.text: Was he tired after the match?
Was he tired after the match

items.hintDe: Mit was/were verwendest du kein did.
items.explainDe: Kein did mit was/were! Die Frage lautet Was he tired …?
items.note: Re-authored from v1 "trip" (untaught) to "match".

prompt.text: You liked the new teacher?

answers.text: Did you like the new teacher?
Did you like the new teacher

items.hintDe: Eine Frage in der Vergangenheit braucht Did am Anfang.
items.explainDe: Fragen in der Vergangenheit brauchen Did + Grundform: Did you like the new teacher?
items.note: verify r1: de-clunked the explainDe compound 'Past-simple-Fragen' to clean German 'Fragen in der Vergangenheit'

prompt.text: You visited the exhibition. (?)

items.hintDe: Mach aus dem Satz eine Frage: Did + Person + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Aus You visited … wird die Frage Did you visit …? – Did vorne, danach die Grundform.

prompt.text: The museum was open. (?)

items.hintDe: Bei was/were stellst du das Verb einfach nach vorne. Kein did!
items.explainDe: Bei be wird aus … was open die Frage Was the museum open? – kein did.

prompt.text: you / have / a good day / at school

answers.text: Did you have a good day at school?
Did you have a good day at school

items.hintDe: Bilde eine Vergangenheitsfrage mit Did.
items.explainDe: Did + Person + Grundform: Did you have a good day at school?

prompt.text: where / you / go / on Saturday

answers.text: Where did you go on Saturday?
Where did you go on Saturday

items.hintDe: Fragewort zuerst, dann did + Person + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Bei W-Fragen steht das Fragewort vorne: Where did you go …? – go bleibt in der Grundform.

prompt.text: Hast du die Geschichte gelesen?

items.hintDe: Frage in der Vergangenheit: Did + du + Grundform von read.
items.explainDe: Did you read …? – nach Did steht die Grundform read (gleich geschrieben wie das Präsens).
items.note: Re-authored from v1 "film" (untaught) to "story".

prompt.text: War Tom gestern krank?

items.hintDe: Bei be (war) fragst du mit Was – kein did.
items.explainDe: war heißt hier Was: Was Tom ill yesterday? – kein did mit was/were.

prompt.text: what / did / you / have / for lunch / ?

answers.text: What did you have for lunch?
What did you have for lunch

items.hintDe: Fragewort, dann did + Person + Grundform.
items.explainDe: What did you have for lunch? – das Fragewort steht vorne, dann did + Grundform.

prompt.text: About the weekend

pairs.left: Did you play in the garden?

items.hintDe: Welche Kurzantwort passt zu welcher Frage? Achte auf did, was und were.
items.explainDe: Kurzantworten wiederholen das Hilfsverb der Frage: Did …? → Yes, I did. / Was …? → Yes, he was. / Were …? → No, they weren't.
items.note: Re-authored from beginnings↔endings (ambiguous cross-matches) to question↔short-answer for unique mapping.

prompt.text: Your friend talks about Saturday. You ask about Tom.

answers.text: Did Tom play football on Saturday?

distractors: Did Tom played football on Saturday?
Was Tom play football on Saturday?
Tom did play football on Saturday?
items.hintDe: Did + Person + Grundform. Welche Frage ist richtig gebaut?
items.explainDe: Richtig ist Did Tom play …? – nach Did steht die Grundform play, nicht played.

distractorPool: Did Tom played football on Saturday?
Was Tom play football on Saturday?
Tom did play football on Saturday?
Did Tom plays football on Saturday?

items.note: "brother" untaught → Tom; "football" alone untaught but probes ok inside the full sentence.

prompt.text: You want to ask a friend about yesterday.

answers.text: Did you have a good day yesterday?
Did you do your homework yesterday?

items.hintDe: Beginne mit Did + you + Grundform und füge yesterday hinzu.
items.explainDe: Eine gute Frage ist z.B. Did you watch TV yesterday? – Did + Person + Grundform.

prompt.text: I ___ listen to the lesson. I was bored.

items.hintDe: Verneinung in der Vergangenheit mit einem normalen Verb.
items.explainDe: Die Verneinung in der Vergangenheit bildest du mit didn't + Grundform: I didn't listen.
items.note: verify r1: strict=true — single-token polarity pair (didn't vs did), the grader must not accept the opposite-meaning near-miss

prompt.text: The sculpture ___ dirty.

items.hintDe: Eine Sache, Vergangenheit, verneint – aber kein didn't bei be!
items.explainDe: be verneinst du mit wasn't/weren't, nicht mit didn't: The sculpture wasn't dirty.
items.note: verify r1: strict=true — polarity pair (wasn't vs was)

prompt.text: We ___ make a cake — we had no time.

items.hintDe: make ist kein be-Verb – welche Verneinung passt?
items.explainDe: Normale Verben verneinst du mit didn't: We didn't make a cake. wasn't/weren't gehören nur zu be.

prompt.text: I didn't listened to a thing.

answers.text: I didn't listen to a thing.
I didn't listen to a thing

items.hintDe: Nach didn't steht die Grundform.
items.explainDe: Nach didn't kommt die Grundform: didn't listen, nicht didn't listened.

prompt.text: The jacket didn't was on the screen.

answers.text: The jacket wasn't on the screen.
The jacket wasn't on the screen

items.hintDe: Mit was/were verwendest du kein didn't.
items.explainDe: Kein didn't mit was/were! Richtig: The jacket wasn't on the screen.

prompt.text: Paul cleaned his room today. (not)

answers.text: Paul didn't clean his room today.
Paul didn't clean his room today
Paul did not clean his room today.
Paul did not clean his room today

items.hintDe: Verneine mit didn't + Grundform. Was passiert mit cleaned?
items.explainDe: In der Verneinung steht die Grundform: Paul didn't clean … – aus cleaned wird wieder clean.
items.note: verify r2 (answers): a free-typed transformation must accept the uncontracted 'did not' form too (matches the tr.001/tr.002 convention); contraction-only would wrongly reject a correct production.

prompt.text: Sie hat den Brief nicht gelesen.

answers.text: She did not read the letter.
She did not read the letter

items.hintDe: Verneinung mit didn't + Grundform von read.
items.explainDe: She didn't read the letter. – nach didn't steht die Grundform read.

prompt.text: didn't or wasn't / weren't?

items.hintDe: Normale Verben → didn't. be (was/were) → wasn't/weren't.
items.explainDe: Normale Verben verneinst du mit didn't, be mit wasn't/weren't.

prompt.text: ___ are you upset? — Because the museum was closed.

items.hintDe: Du fragst nach dem Grund.
items.explainDe: Mit Why fragst du nach dem Grund: Why are you upset?

prompt.text: Why do you like art? — ___ it is great.

items.hintDe: Du gibst einen Grund an.
items.explainDe: Den Grund leitest du mit Because ein: Because it is great.
items.note: Re-authored from SB "sport is cool" (sport+cool untaught) to "art ... great".

prompt.text: ___ is the garden a mess? — Because there were lots of people there.

items.hintDe: Welches Fragewort fragt nach dem Grund?
items.explainDe: Nach dem Grund fragst du mit Why. Because gibt den Grund – es steht in der Antwort, nicht in der Frage.

prompt.text: Because art great is.

items.hintDe: Nach because steht ein normaler Satz: Subjekt + Verb + Rest.
items.explainDe: Im Englischen steht das Verb nicht am Satzende: Because art is great.
items.note: Targets the L1-transfer verb-final error from the brief; "sport is cool" untaught → "art is great".

prompt.text: Why and because

pairs.right: Because a neighbour called the police.

pairs.left: Why is the garden a mess?
pairs.right: Because there were lots of people there.

items.hintDe: Welcher Grund passt zu welcher Why-Frage?
items.explainDe: Jede Antwort beginnt mit Because und nennt den passenden Grund zur Frage.

prompt.text: because / I / this one / don't / like

answers.text: Because I don't like this one.
Because I don't like this one

items.hintDe: Nach because: Subjekt + Verb in normaler Reihenfolge.
items.explainDe: Nach because folgt ein ganzer Satz: Because I don't like this one.

prompt.text: Warum bist du so glücklich? — Weil ich einen Hund habe.

answers.text: Why are you so happy? — Because I have a dog.
Why are you so happy? Because I have a dog.
Why are you so happy? — Because I have got a dog.

items.hintDe: Frag mit Why, antworte mit Because + ganzer Satz.
items.explainDe: Why are you so happy? – Because I have a dog. Nach Because kommt Subjekt + Verb.
